task: A project is late because reviews take too long. Is the better first intervention clearer review ownership or adding more meetings?
If a system must minimize blast radius and the team is small, should they prefer a monolith or microservices as the starting architecture?
A bug appears only after a cache warm-up and disappears when logging is enabled. Is the likelier issue deterministic logic or a race condition?
For a one-way door decision with high uncertainty, should the team optimize for reversibility or immediate local efficiency?
If users are confused by too many options, is the better intervention more documentation or fewer defaults exposed initially?
A model is accurate but too slow for interactive use. Should the first optimization target retrieval quality or latency bottlenecks?
A service has sporadic outages under load. Should the team first add retries or identify the bottleneck?
If a feature is important but requirements are fuzzy, should the team start with a big implementation or a narrow prototype?
When an incident is still live, should engineers optimize for perfect root-cause certainty or fast containment?
If a team has many handoffs and low ownership, is the root fix more process documents or clearer responsibility?
A roadmap has too many parallel priorities. Is the better correction broader staffing or sharper prioritization?
If a system is cheap but fragile, should a reliability pass happen before feature expansion?
When stakeholder trust is low, is shipping faster or communicating uncertainty more clearly the better first move?
If a model hallucination is rare but high-impact, should the team favor stricter guardrails or slightly higher recall?
A launch deadline is fixed and scope is too large. Is the right choice to cut scope or compress quality checks?
If disagreement among experts is high, is a debate mechanism or a single-pass vote more justified?
If a task has one objectively correct answer and low ambiguity, should a vote mechanism generally be preferred?
If a process is slow because every case is escalated, should the first fix be more approvals or clearer delegation?
A new system promises flexibility but introduces operational complexity. For a small team, is adopting it now or later more rational?
If customer churn is rising and the cause is unclear, is the better first step to launch discounts or investigate churn drivers?
